steps.prompt: Which is the main finite verb?

hints.haec: 'haec' is a demonstrative pronoun meaning 'these things'
hints.meminisse: 'meminisse' is an infinitive meaning 'to remember' — it is not the finite verb

reveal.gloss: it will help / it will please

hints.Present: Present of 'iuvare' 3rd person would be 'iuvat' — 'iuvabit' has the -bi- future marker
hints.Imperfect: Imperfect of 'iuvare' would be 'iuvabat' — 'iuvabit' has -bit, the future ending
hints.Perfect: Perfect of 'iuvare' would be 'iuvit' — 'iuvabit' has the -bi- future marker

hints.iubeo, iubere, iussi, iussum: These are the principal parts of 'iubere' (to order)
hints.iungo, iungere, iunxi, iunctum: These are the principal parts of 'iungere' (to join)
hints.iudico, iudicare, iudicavi, iudicatum: These are the principal parts of 'iudicare' (to judge)

hints.to remind: 'to remind' is 'admonere' — 'meminisse' is the perfect infinitive of 'memini' (to remember)
hints.to forget: 'to forget' is 'oblivisci' — 'meminisse' means the opposite: to remember
hints.to think: 'to think' is 'cogitare' or 'putare' — 'meminisse' specifically means to remember

steps.target: It is a defective verb — only has perfect-system forms
choices: It is a defective verb — only has perfect-system forms

hints.It is a deponent verb: Deponent verbs have passive forms with active meaning — 'memini' has active perfect forms with present meaning
hints.It is an impersonal verb: Impersonal verbs only appear in 3rd person — 'memini' can be conjugated in all persons
hints.It is a semi-deponent verb: Semi-deponents are active in present, passive in perfect — 'memini' only has perfect-stem forms

hints.Nominative: While 'haec' can be nominative feminine singular or neuter plural, here it is the object of 'meminisse'
hints.Genitive: Genitive of 'hic' would be 'huius' — 'haec' is nominative or accusative neuter plural
hints.Ablative: Ablative of 'hic' neuter plural would be 'his' — 'haec' is accusative neuter plural here

hints.now: 'now' is 'nunc' — 'olim' refers to a different time, either past or future

translation: Perhaps someday it will help to remember these things
